key_insight: A multi-scale ML framework couples chemical, spin, and structural disorder to predict phase behavior in disordered alloys.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable design and screening of complex magnetic alloys and other multi-disorder materials for structural, thermal, and spintronic applications.
method: Combine graph neural networks and machine-learning interatomic potentials.
Use Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics to sample coupled disorder across scales.
Model Fe-Co-C as a test case with chemical, spin, and interstitial structural disorder.
evidence_signal: The framework predicts Fe-Co order-to-disorder and melting temperatures of 1,000 K and 1,690 K, matching experiment at 1,006 K and about 1,700 K, and captures tetragonal-to-nearly-cubic transitions in Fe-Co-C.
watch_item: Accuracy may depend on high-quality training data and careful coupling of magnetic, chemical, and structural interactions across scales.
paper_title: A Multi-Scale Machine Learning Framework for Coupled Chemical, Spin, and Structural Disorder in Alloys
